mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals persistent labor market expansion that increases aggregate wage income and consumption expenditure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132912.0 thousands in latest print
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS change and 20-day returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint eliminates risk-free yield, forcing portfolio rebalancing into equities and compressing equity discount rates, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the mean return during triggered periods does not exceed the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales and rising unemployment creates a joint demand-labor market stress signal that amplifies recession probabilities and triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) relative to individual series.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 10%, month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate signal accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household spending and corporate revenue expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 10% and rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive earnings surprises in the industrial and materials sectors and lifts aggregate S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increases, or the Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and 60-day returns is below 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, accelerating consumer prices erode real corporate margins and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of accommodative monetary policy indicated by a federal funds rate below 1% and month-over-month growth in housing starts generates a credit-construction accelerator that boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 1% and HOUST increases month-over-month, or the linear interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP signals a recessionary decline in aggregate demand that reduces top-line revenue growth across the corporate sector, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 contracts quarter-over-quarter.
